scope: Extract and export a fast operator shortlist from the deeper 180-domain frontier checkpoint

decision_context.prior_state: The deeper 180-domain frontier checkpoint became the best breadth read, but the full ranked export was still too large for quick operator inspection.
decision_context.decision: Build a shortlist layer from the deeper 180 readout instead of opening another expensive frontier run immediately.
decision_context.why_now: This makes the current simulator winners visible in a smaller, action-ready surface while preserving the broader checkpoint underneath.

files_expected.purpose: Structured shortlist packet split into winners, breakouts, and speculative domains.
Operator-facing markdown shortlist export for the deeper 180 frontier checkpoint.
Documents why the shortlist exists and how it should be used.

planned_steps: Build a shortlist packet from the deeper 180 frontier readout
Format the shortlist as a markdown operator export
Save the tranche note and day-level diff summary update

commands: $env:PYTHONPATH='src'; python -m pytest tests/test_mirofish_hybrid.py tests/test_watchtower.py tests/test_mirofish_discovery.py tests/test_mirofish_portfolio.py tests/test_trend_prediction.py -q

$env:PYTHONPATH='src'; python -m chip_labs.cli mirofish-frontier-shortlist-export --input research/meta/MIROFISH_FRONTIER_SHORTLIST_180_DEEPER_2026-03-25.json --output research/meta/MIROFISH_FRONTIER_SHORTLIST_180_DEEPER_2026-03-25.md --title "MiroFish Frontier Shortlist 180 Deeper"
expected_checks: The shortlist cleanly separates winners, breakouts, and speculative frontier domains
The deeper 180 checkpoint has a smaller operator surface for review without changing the underlying simulation result
Top retained and top choice frontier domains remain visible without reopening another run

guardrails: Do not mutate frontier conversion logic in this tranche
Do not replace the underlying deeper 180 checkpoint with the shortlist
Use the shortlist as an operator layer, not as an auto-promotion decision
